rules.label: Rust Check Then Open Path
rules.description: Filesystem code that checks metadata or existence before opening a path.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Check Then Open Path matters because the pattern here, Filesystem code that checks metadata or existence before opening a path, is worth review because crate and module edges are where Rust projects either clarify ownership or hide it.
rules.fix: For Rust Check Then Open Path in Rust, keep boundary contracts explicit and avoid leaking implementation details across module lines without a strong reason.

rules.label: Rust Internal Anyhow Result
rules.description: Internal library functions that return anyhow-style error surfaces instead of crate-local errors.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Internal Anyhow Result matters because the pattern here, Internal library functions that return anyhow-style error surfaces instead of crate-local errors, shows that the code is bypassing a clearer or safer default that readers usually expect to see.
rules.fix: For Rust Internal Anyhow Result in Rust, prefer crate-local errors when the semantics match, and keep the expected or cheaper API choice as the default.

rules.label: Rust Narrowing Numeric Cast
rules.description: Numeric narrowing casts that may silently truncate or change precision.

rules.label: Rust Secret Equality Compare
rules.description: Direct equality or inequality comparisons on secret-like values.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Secret Equality Compare matters because the pattern here, Direct equality or inequality comparisons on secret-like values, hides a sensitive edge where convenience shortcuts can become security or data-handling incidents.
rules.fix: For Rust Secret Equality Compare in Rust, pull the sensitive value from env, config, or a secret manager instead of embedding it directly in source.

rules.label: Rust Unbounded Read to String
rules.description: Production code that reads an entire file into a string without a size bound.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Unbounded Read to String matters because the pattern here, Production code that reads an entire file into a string without a size bound, removes a guardrail or signal that reviewers expect before trusting the surrounding code.
rules.fix: For Rust Unbounded Read to String in Rust, add a size bound or move the code to a path where that safeguard is guaranteed before the risky work happens.
